components.name: Progress
components.description: Determinate progress bar showing completion percentage. Track is 6px tall with rounded-full corners. Indicator fills from left. Optional label row shows title + percentage above the bar. Type property controls indicator color — Primary (default) or Chart 1–8 for dashboard KPI colors.

examples.name: Chart Colors
examples.description: Chart-colored progress bars for dashboards — each type maps to a chart color token.

examples.name: With Label
examples.description: Progress bar with title and percentage label above.

props.name: value
props.description: Completion percentage (0–100). Controls indicator width via translateX. Null/undefined = indeterminate.

props.name: type
props.description: Indicator color — primary (default) or chart-1..8 for dashboard KPI colors

props.name: className
props.description: Tailwind classes for the track — e.g. 'h-2' for thicker bar or 'bg-secondary' for custom track color

bestPractices.do: Always pair with a label or aria-label so users know what is progressing — 'Uploading file… 45%'.
bestPractices.dont: Show a progress bar without any context — users won't know what it represents.

bestPractices.do: Use type prop for chart-colored bars in dashboards (sales channels, KPIs). Keep primary for standard progress flows.
bestPractices.dont: Mix chart colors randomly — use consistent type per metric across the app.

keyboard.action: Progress is not interactive — no keyboard interaction

notes: Radix Progress renders role='progressbar' with aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin=0, aria-valuemax=100 automatically.
Add aria-label or aria-labelledby when no visible label is present — e.g. aria-label='Upload progress'.
Screen readers announce value changes — avoid rapid updates (debounce to ~500ms intervals).

related.name: Spinner
related.desc: Indeterminate loading indicator. Use Spinner when completion percentage is unknown; Progress when it's measurable.

related.name: Skeleton
related.desc: Layout-aware loading placeholder. Use Skeleton for content loading; Progress for task completion.
